reason: All four acceptance criteria are fully met. (1) NFKC normalization is applied via unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', text) in Step 1. (2) Non-printable control characters are removed by checking unicodedata.category(ch)=='Cc' and excluding only U+000A (\n) and U+0009 (\t). (3) text.strip() is applied after cleaning. (4) No pattern matching is performed — the implementation explicitly delegates that to L3. Tests cover NFKC fullwidth normalization, combining-character composition, NUL/control removal, \n/\t preservation, leading/trailing whitespace stripping, and empty string. The ValueError on malformed Unicode is a defensive addition not required by the SRS but not in conflict with it. Test excerpt truncates before test_empty_string body but the function signature and docstring are present and consistent with the criterion.
